* Layer:   E2E (real Chromium browser, running Next.js server)
* Pattern: Page Object Model (LoginPage) + custom fixtures
*   E2E tests verify user-facing behaviour in a REAL browser.
*   They do NOT substitute for unit/component tests — each layer has a job:
*     E2E       → full stack integration + real browser quirks + accessibility tree
*   ✅ Use Page Object Model — no raw locators in this file
*   ✅ No waitForTimeout — only deterministic waits (expect().toBeVisible(), etc.)
*   ✅ Each test is fully independent — no ordering dependency
*   ✅ Descriptive test names (reads like a specification)

* Shared auth API mock helpers for E2E tests.
* QA principle: Auth API contracts should be defined in ONE place.
* Page objects call these helpers instead of defining responses inline.
* Shared phone number fixtures for E2E tests.
* QA principle: E2E tests should not import from feature __tests__ directories.
* This file provides the same test data as features/auth/login/__tests__/fixtures/phones.ts
* but is owned by the E2E test layer.
* QA principle: Locators and user actions live HERE, not inside test files.
* Test files describe WHAT to verify. This file describes HOW to interact.
*   - Single place to update when the UI changes
*   - Shared across all E2E tests that touch the login page
